coredata.dc:title: A novel self-assembled dual-emissive ratiometric fluorescent nanoprobe for alkaline phosphatase sensing
coredata.dc:creator: Han, Zhixuan
Wang, Nan
Lv, Yuntai
Fu, Qingjie
Wang, Guannan
Su, Xingguang
coredata.dc:description:
Background Alkaline phosphatase (ALP) is widely found in various organs and tissues of the human body which could assist in the verification of the presence of various diseases through its content in the blood. In the past few years, many analytical methods for ALP activity assays have been explored. However, a simple and economical method with high sensitivity and specificity also remains great challenge. Therefore, the development of sensitive and efficient approach for ALP analysis is of great significance in biomedical studies.
Results Herein, we constructed a highly sensitive and label-free ratiometric fluorometric biosensing platform for the determination of ALP activity, which utilizing lysozyme(Ly)-functionalized 5-methyl-2-thiouracil(MTU)-modified gold nanoclusters (MTU-Ly@Au NC) and poly-dopamine (PDA) as signal indicators. Dopamine (DA) can self-polymerizes to form PDA under alkaline conditions that can further quenched the fluorescence of MTU-Ly@Au NC at 525 nm due to fluorescence resonance energy transfer (FRET) and absorption competition quenching (ACQ) effects. In this process, the PDA fluorescence intensity at 325 nm was nearly unchanged. After the addition of ALP, ascorbic acid (AA) which can alleviate the self-polymerization process of DA was generated from the substrate ascorbic acid 2-phosphate (AAP), thus changing ratiometric fluorescence intensity of I525/I325. Hence, by monitoring the fluorescence ratio (I525/I325), a ratiometric fluorescence biosensing platform for ALP was established with the linear calibration in the range of 0.5–8 U L−1 and the limit of detection of 0.157 U L−1.
Significance This work not only synthesized a novel fluorescence probe with simple preparation and low cost for ALP which has excellent anti-interference properties and selectivity. Furthermore, this biosensing platform was successfully applied for the determination of ALP activity in human serum samples. This work provided a potential tool for biomedical diagnostics in the future.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Ratiometric fluorescence
Polydopamine
Gold nanoclusters
Alkaline phosphatase

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Assay of ALP activity in serum samples
Characterization of MTU-Ly@Au NCs and PDA

National Natural Science Foundation of China

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (a) The synthesis of MTU-Ly@Au NC (b) Fluorescence excitation, emission and UV–visible absorption spectra of MTU-Ly@Au NC. (c) Fluorescence emission spectra of MTU-Ly@Au NC under different excitation conditions. (d) Fluorescence excitation, emission and UV–visible absorption spectra of PDA. (e) UV–vis absorption spectra of DA and PDA (f) UV–visible absorption spectra of PDA in different self-polymerization time.

Infrared spectra (a) of the MTU-Ly@Au NC. (b) XPS survey spectrum of MTU-Ly@Au NC. XPS spectra of Au 4f (c) and S 2p (d) in MTU-Ly@Au NC. TEM map of MTU@Au NC (e) and MTU-Ly@Au NC (f), with the inset showing its particle size distribution.

(a) Fluorescence spectra of the PDA, MTU-Ly@Au NC, PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC, AA/PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC, AAP/ALP/PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC systems. (b)Overlap portions of the PDA absorption spectra with the MTU-Ly@Au NC emission spectra. (c)Overlap of the UV absorption spectra of PDA and MTU-Ly@Au NC (d)Fluorescence lifetime profiles of MTU-Ly@Au NC and PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC.

(a) Fluorescence spectra of PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC system with the addition of AA (50, 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 800 1000 μmol L−1). (b) Linear plot of the fluorescence intensity ratio (I525/I325) and AA concentration. (c) Fluorescence spectra of PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC/AAP system after different levels of ALP treatment (0.5, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8 U L−1). (d) Linear plot of the fluorescence intensity ratio (I525/I325) and ALP concentration.

(a) Selectivity of the PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC/AAP system for ALP analysis. (b) Anti-interference assay of PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC/AAP system to different coexisting interfering substances. Concentration: 5 U·L−1 ALP, 10 U L−1 other bio-enzymes, 50 μM small biological molecules as well as amino acid, 100 μM common ions.
Schematic representation of fluorescence sensing strategy for AA/ALP analysis based on MTU-Ly@Au NCs/PDA.
Assay results for ALP determination in serum sample (n = 3).

ce:table-footnote.ce:label: a
ce:table-footnote.ce:note-para: The serum samples were diluted 100 times.

head.ce:title: A novel self-assembled dual-emissive ratiometric fluorescent nanoprobe for alkaline phosphatase sensing

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Department of Analytical Chemistry, College of Chemistry, Jilin University, Changchun, 130012, China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Department of Analytical Chemistry, College of Chemistry, Jilin University, Changchun 130012, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Pharmacy, Shenyang Medical University, Shenyang, 110034, China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Pharmacy, Shenyang Medical University, Shenyang, 110034, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Alkaline phosphatase (ALP) is widely found in various organs and tissues of the human body which could assist in the verification of the presence of various diseases through its content in the blood. In the past few years, many analytical methods for ALP activity assays have been explored. However, a simple and economical method with high sensitivity and specificity also remains great challenge. Therefore, the development of sensitive and efficient approach for ALP analysis is of great significance in biomedical studies.

Herein, we constructed a highly sensitive and label-free ratiometric fluorometric biosensing platform for the determination of ALP activity, which utilizing lysozyme(Ly)-functionalized 5-methyl-2-thiouracil(MTU)-modified gold nanoclusters (MTU-Ly@Au NC) and poly-dopamine (PDA) as signal indicators. Dopamine (DA) can self-polymerizes to form PDA under alkaline conditions that can further quenched the fluorescence of MTU-Ly@Au NC at 525 nm due to fluorescence resonance energy transfer (FRET) and absorption competition quenching (ACQ) effects. In this process, the PDA fluorescence intensity at 325 nm was nearly unchanged. After the addition of ALP, ascorbic acid (AA) which can alleviate the self-polymerization process of DA was generated from the substrate ascorbic acid 2-phosphate (AAP), thus changing ratiometric fluorescence intensity of I525/I325. Hence, by monitoring the fluorescence ratio (I525/I325), a ratiometric fluorescence biosensing platform for ALP was established with the linear calibration in the range of 0.5–8 U L−1 and the limit of detection of 0.157 U L−1.

This work not only synthesized a novel fluorescence probe with simple preparation and low cost for ALP which has excellent anti-interference properties and selectivity. Furthermore, this biosensing platform was successfully applied for the determination of ALP activity in human serum samples. This work provided a potential tool for biomedical diagnostics in the future.

In this study, after the combination of MTU-Ly@Au NCs and polydopamine (PDA), the ratiometric fluorescence of the system will be changed due to FRET and ACQ effects. DA self-polymerization can be effectively inhibited after AA addition. AAP can generate AA under the catalysis of ALP, thus, AA concentration and ALP activity could be directly detected by monitoring ratios fluorescence intensities of PDA and MTU-Ly@Au NCs.
Image 1

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: ALP as a hydrolase enzyme which could convert substrate AAP to AA is closely related to osteoblast differentiation, proliferation and bone mineralization [1,2]. In addition, abnormal high-load endogenous ALP is linked with numerous diseases, including liver dysfunction, bone as well as intestinal disease [3]. Therefore, developing sensitive and efficient approaches for ALP analysis is of outstanding significance. Besides, due to its potent ability to move phosphate groups, it is also widely used in molecular biology and enzymatic immunoassays [3,4]. Compared with colorimetric and other methods, fluorimetric method receives special attention due to its inherent merits of high sensitivity, easy operation and fast response speed [5–7]. As a result, the design of novel fluorescence probe with simple preparation and low cost for ALP is of great need in biomedical studies.

As novel fluorescent nanomaterials, metal nanoclusters (NCs) with distinctive subnanosize, adjustable fluorescence emission, and excellent bio-compatibility, have received much attention for catalysis, sensing, and bio-imaging applications [8,9]. Currently, numerous fluorescent NCs materials have been designed, the dimension, structure as well as composition of these NCs could be tuned by the bonding ability of metal-metal and metal-ligand [10–13]. Gold nanoclusters (AuNC) has outstanding properties such as low cytotoxicity, high photostability and biocompatibility. In contrast, silver nanoclusters (AgNC) are susceptible to atmospheric oxygen and other electron receptors in solution and have a strong agglomeration tendency to be less stable than the AuNC. Copper nanoclusters (CuNC) are easy to oxidation and difficult to prepare small particles [15]. Therefore, AuNC has an important role in fluorescence detection and biomolecular labeling [14,16]. Wang et al. prepared a gold nanocluster functionalized with dual proteins (BL-Au NCs) to detect polynucleotide kinase [16]. The concordancy of AuNC and biomolecules bring appreciable synergism, especially proteins with rich amino acid residues which could protect NCs effectively and improve their optical properties [17–19].

In this study, we prepared MTU-Ly@Au NC by combining lysozyme (Ly) with MTU onto gold nanoclusters by one-pot method. After the combination of MTU-Ly@Au NCs and polydopamine (PDA) which are obtained by DA self-polymerization under alkaline conditions, the ratiometric fluorescence of the system will be changed due to FRET and ACQ effects. DA self-polymerization can be effectively inhibited after AA addition. AAP can generate AA under the catalysis of ALP, thus, AA concentration and ALP activity could be directly detected by monitoring ratios fluorescence intensities of PDA at 325 and MTU-Ly@Au NCs at 525 nm (Scheme 1 ). We achieved a sensitive and accurate detection of ALP based MTU-Ly@Au NC/PDA ratiometric fluorescence analysis platform.
Referring to the supporting information for chemicals and instruments.

The MTU-Ly@Au NC was synthesized based on our previous work [20,22,23]. Firstly, 200 μL HAuCl4 (25 mmol L−1) was added to a mixed solution of 180 μL MTU (50 mmol L−1) and 20 μL of sodium hydroxide (NaOH, 1.4 mol L−1), after 8 h of stirring at room temperature. Subsequently, 200 μL of freshly prepared lysozyme solution (20 mg mL−1) was added. After 4 h of stirring at room temperature, the yellow emitting MTU-Ly@Au NC was obtained. The fluorescence quantum yield of MTU-Ly@Au NC was determined to be 19.93 % [20].

For the detection of AA, 100 μL 8 mM of dopamine, 100 μL glycine-NaOH (5 mmol L−1, pH 10.4), 20 μL MTU-Ly@Au NC and 100 μL different concentrations of AA were mixed and stirred for 30min at room temperature, ultrapure water was added to gain a 1 mL solution to obtain a fluorescence emission spectrum with an excitation light of 300 nm.

For the detection of ALP, 100 μL of different concentrations of ALP, 100 μL 2 mmol·L−1AAP and 10 μL Tris-HCl (pH 9.0,100 mM) were incubated in a 37 °C water-bath for 30 min. Then, 100 μL 8 mM dopamine, 100 μL glycine-NaOH (5 mmol L−1, pH 10.4), 20 μL MTU-Ly@Au NC were added to the above solution, after mixing and stirring for 30 min, ultrapure water was added to gain a 1 mL solution to obtain a fluorescence emission spectrum with an excitation light of 300 nm.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Assay of ALP activity in serum samples
ce:section.ce:para: Blood samples of healthy adults were obtained from China−Japan Union Hospital. Serum samples were gain from centrifugation at 11 200 rpm for 30 min. Then, the detection of ALP followed the above procedure under the premise of taking diluted serum samples instead of water.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Characterization of MTU-Ly@Au NCs and PDA
ce:section.ce:para: The MTU-Ly@Au NC nanoprobes were prepared by a simple one-pot synthesis, and procedures were described in Fig. 1 a. As shown in Fig. 1b, the fluorescence excitation spectrum of MTU-Ly@Au NC have two characteristic peaks of 321 nm and 430 nm. The characteristic peak at 321 nm is result from the π-π * transition [24], and 430 nm is the n-π * transition [25,26]. The fluorescence emission spectrum has only one emission peak at 550 nm, and this double excitation-single emission phenomenon is owing from hyperconjugation action in its dual-fluorescence-center [20,27]. The fluorescence emission peak of MTU-Ly@Au NC does not change under different excitation wavelengths (Fig. 1c). As shown in Fig. 1d, the fluorescence excitation and emission of PDA were individually located at 280 nm and 325 nm. The UV–visible absorption spectrum of PDA showed an absorption peak at 285 nm. The CIE data simulations also match with the PDA and MTU-Ly@Au NC fluorescence emission profiles (Fig. S1). Fig. 1e displayed that after adding DA into an alkaline environment, the characteristic absorption peak of DA at 280 red shifted to 285 nm, and the absorbance intensity is greatly improved, which was due to the formation of PDA. With the increase of the DA self-polymerization time from 1min to 30min, the absorbance in the range of 300–600 nm increased continuously, and the absorbance changed little after 30min, which demonstrates that the self-polymerization of DA is almost completed in 30min at alkaline environment (Fig. 1f).

Lysozyme is a biological small molecule containing a large number of guanidine group. It is often used as a stabilizer for nanoclusters and metal nanoparticles [29–31]. We prepared MTU-Ly@Au NC by combining lysozyme with MTU-modified gold nanoclusters. The guanidinium group of lysozyme binds to CO and N–H of MTU to form hydrogen bonds, which has already been demonstrated in our previous work [23]. Due to hydrogen bonding, MTU-Ly@Au NC is likely to have superior stability and fluorescence properties when compared to MTU@Au NC [32–34]. The fluorescence intensity of MTU-Ly@Au NC increased by 3.6-fold compared to MTU@Au NC (Fig. S2). The FT-IR spectra show that MTU-Ly@Au NC has characteristic peaks of 1670 cm−1,1540 cm−1,1470 cm−1 and 1360 cm−1, corresponding to the stretching vibrations of CO, N–H, CN as well as C–N. In contrast to the MTU@Au NC, the intensity of the characteristic peak of MTU-Ly@Au NC at 1540 cm−1 increased, and the characteristic peak at 1670 cm−1 move towards high wavenumbers, indicating that the combination of Ly and gold cluster generates a new bond (Fig. 2 a). The X-ray photoelectron spectra (XPS) in Fig. 2b and Fig. S3 show that the Au, C, N, O, and S elements are the main constituent elements of MTU-Ly@Au NC. As shown in Fig. 2c, the 4f track of Au is split into two parts at 84.68 eV and 88.28 eV: the former consists of 4f7/2 (85.10 eV) of Au(I) and 4f7/2 (84.20 eV) of Au(0), the latter consists of 4f5/2 (88.60 eV) of Au(I) and 4f5/2 (87.94 eV) of Au(0) [35–37]. Meanwhile, the S 2p spectrum in Fig. 2d divided into three peaks, oxidized S (165.30 eV), unbound S (163.93 eV) as well as Au–S (163.03 eV), indicating that MTU provides the thiol group and forms an Au–S bond with Au (I) [22,38,39]. The average particle size of MTU-Ly@Au NC is 1.88 nm according to TEM (Fig. 2f), which is larger than 1.06 nm of MTU@Au NC (Fig. 2e), therefore, further proving the successful synthesis of MTU-Ly@Au NC.

As shown in Fig. 3 a, after addition of MTU-Ly@Au NC to PDA system, the fluorescence peak of MTU-Ly@Au NC was blue-shifted owing to the π-π stacking interactions between PDA and MTU-Ly@Au NC, which can alter the chemical environment of MTU-Ly@Au NC, and leading to the occurrence of a blue-shift [23,28]. The fluorescence intensity of both PDA and MTU-Ly@Au NC decreased significantly after 1min of mixing, and the MTU-Ly@Au NC was further quenched after 30min mixing, while the fluorescence of PDA almost kept unchanged. Both FRET and ACQ has the role of quenching for MTU-Ly@Au NC, thus, its fluorescence gradually decreased. After addition of AA, the fluorescent of MTU-Ly@Au NC partially recovered which was attributed to AA hindering the self-polymerization of PDA, ALP enzymes can catalyze the substrate AAP to generate AA, therefore, the addition of ALP and AAP can also hinder PDA generation. The absorption spectrum of PDA overlaps with the emission spectrum of MTU-Ly@Au NC (Fig. 3b), which provides a prerequisite for FRET [40–42].

As can be seen in Fig. 3c, both PDA and MTU-Ly@Au NC have a strong absorbance and the ranges overlap with each other which provides the conditions for the occurrence of the ACQ effect [43–46]. In addition, the position of the absorption characteristic peaks of PDA and MTU-Ly@Au NC does not changed after mixing (Fig. S4), so the static quenching mechanism can be preliminarily excluded [47]. The fluorescence lifetime of MTU-Ly@Au NC is decreased after mixing of PDA and MTU-Ly@Au NC (Fig. 3d), further illustrating the occurrence of FRET [47,48]. Furthermore, the distance between PDA and MTU-Ly@Au NC was calculated to be 7.28 nm (Supporting Information), which meets the requirements of FRET [49]. So, the fluorescence quenching of the MT-LZ@GNC by PDA is caused by the ACQ and FRET. When AA is present in the system, Michael addition reaction occurs between AA and DA [21], and their combination has a higher priority than the DA self-polymerization reaction, thus, DA self-polymerization can be effectively inhibited after AA addition. AAP can generate AA under the catalysis of ALP, so AA concentration and ALP activity could be directly detected by monitoring the ratio fluorescence intensities of PDA and MTU-Ly@Au NC.

Before the quantitative determination of AA and ALP, some key parameters were optimized. The fluorescence of MTU-Ly@Au NC was mostly quenched within 30min by PDA (Fig. S5). After the addition of AA, the quenching of MTU-Ly@AuNC was relieved within 30min. The CIE data simulation also support the effect of AA on MTU-Ly@Au NC/PDA system (Fig. S6). Therefore, 30min reaction time was chosen for further experiments. Under alkaline conditions, DA will be oxidized and self-polymerization to form PDA, and different pH environment can also affect the hindering effect of AA on DA self-polymerization [50]. The optimal pH for AA hindering DA self-polymerization should be explored within the appropriate pH range for DA self-polymerization. We performed the pH optimization of the PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC and AA/PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC system in the range of pH 8.5–11, it was found that although the trend of the ratiometric fluorescence intensity I525/I325 of the two systems all decreased with pH increase, but I525nm/I325nm was increased in the range of pH 10.2–10.4 for AA/PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC system, while the elevated trend did not occur for PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC system. These results indicated that when pH is too low, the DA self-polymerization speed is very slow, and the hindering effect of AA is small. When pH is too high, the DA self-polymerization is too fast, and AA obstruction has no effect. Therefore, pH 10.4 is the optimal reaction condition, in which AA can greatly hinder the occurrence of DA self-polymerization and thus cause the increase of I525/I325(Fig. S7). We have also performed the optimization of the ALP reaction temperature, Fig. S8 shows that the ratiometric fluorescent intensity I525/I325 almost unchanged within the 25–45 °C range. To meet the physiological environment, the reaction temperature of 37 °C for AAP and ALP was chosen. We also optimized the incubation time for the enzymatic reaction of AAP catalyzed by ALP and 30min was chosen for the further experiments (Fig. S9).

The fluorescence quenching of the MT-LZ@GNC is caused by PDA via the ACQ and FRET, whose essence is derived from the concentration of the PDA, in other words, the speed of PDA generation. Therefore, AA concentration and reaction time dominant the concentration of PDA, the latter in turn determine the degree of quenching of MTU-Ly@Au NC and finally determine the ratiometric fluorescence of I525/I325. Under the optimal conditions, the fluorescence emission profiles of PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC systems with different AA concentrations were shown in Fig. 4 a. When the concentration of AA raised, fluorescence intensity at 525 nm increased and 325 nm maintained constant. Meanwhile, as shown in Fig. 4b, the AA concentration was found to be linear with I525/I325, and the regression equation was I525/I325 = 0.0025 [AA] + 1.06 (R2 = 0.986) within the range of 50–1000 μM with a limit of detection (LOD) at 17.2 μM calculated by 3σ/slope (σ is the standard deviation of the blank signal (n = 11). The fluorescence spectra of PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC/AAP system with different levels of ALP were shown in Fig. 4c.The ALP concentration was found to be linear with I525/I325, and the regression equation was I525/I325 = 0.35 [ALP] + 0.81 (R2 = 0.989) with a LOD of 0.157 U L−1 within the range of 0.5–8 U L−1 (Fig. 4d). The relative standard deviations (RSD) of ALP detection at 4 U L−1 were 3.21 % in six replicates which indicated an excellent reproducibility of our methods. As shown in Table S1, this strategy yields a competent linear range, and the LOD is lower than most previously reported methods, indicating that this strategy has high sensitivity for ALP analysis.

For complex biological sample, both selectivity and anti-interference were assumed as important features for evaluating the utility of sensing platforms. For this reason, we replaced ALP with biological enzymes, biological small molecules, amino acids and common ions to explore the method selectivity. We also performed anti-interference experiments by simultaneously adding ALP and other substances to the system. The I525/I325 of PDA/MTU-Ly@Au NC/AAP system after the ALP addition was significantly higher than other substances, demonstrating the great selectivity of the system (Fig. 5 a). The interference assay (Fig. 5b) demonstrated that ALP is less affected when coexisting with other substances. These observations show that the sensing platform has a significant anti-interference capacity for the detection of ALP activity.

From an application perspective, monitoring the ALP activity in real samples has great practical value. Human serum was used as a model matrix to measure the application of the strategy for ALP detection. ALP serum levels in healthy adults were at the range of 40–150 U L−1, and serum were diluted before the experiment to prevent going beyond the test range [51]. The recovery is between 93.35 and 106.3 %, RSD is within 3.7 % (Table 1 ). AA generated by the ALP enzyme catalyzing the substrate AAP could bind to o-Phenylenediamine (OPD) under alkaline conditions to generate 3-(1,2-dihydroxyethyl)furo [3,4b]-quinoxaline (QXD), and QXD concentration is linearly related to ALP activity (Fig. S10), therefore the OPD assay can be served as a control experiment of our detection method [52]. Table 1 shown that the results of ALP assay in the serum samples are similar with OPD assay method demonstrating the practicality and accuracy of the detection strategy (Table 1).

In this work, we constructed a sensitive detection strategy for ALP by combining MTU-Ly@Au NC with PDA. DA can self-polymerize to form PDA under alkaline conditions, and fluorescence of MTU-Ly@Au NC was quenched by PDA owing to FRET and ACQ effects. AA preferentially binds to DA due to the Michael addition reaction which would hinder the formation of PDA. ALP could catalyze AAP to generate AA, so the determination of ALP could be achieved by monitoring the change of the fluorescence intensity ratio of MTU-Ly@Au NC and PDA. Moreover, the application of this approach was also demonstrated in real sample determination, providing a promising candidate for the biochemical research as well as medical applications of ALP.

Zhixuan Han: Conceptualization, Data curation, Formal analysis, Investigation, Methodology, Resources, Software, Validation, Visualization, Writing – original draft, Writing – review & editing. Nan Wang: Data curation. Yuntai Lv: Resources. Qingjie Fu: Investigation. Guannan Wang: Supervision. Xingguang Su: Funding acquisition, Project administration, Supervision.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work is supported by the National Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 22374058), the Science and Technology Development project of Jilin province, China (No. 20220402001 GH), the Open Funds of the State Key Laboratory of Electroanalytical Chemistry, China (No. SKLEAC202201).
